what_it_does: Defines a browser global type registry for ConceptV Studio, with in-memory built-in, user, and template maps, localStorage persistence for user/template records, query helpers, inheritance resolution, slot acceptance checks, normalization, and subscription notifications.
observations: Runs as an IIFE and publishes its API on window.CV_REGISTRY.
Defines fixed layer names and per-layer display metadata including label, rank, swatch, and description.
Keeps built-in records in memory while persisting user records and templates to separate localStorage keys.
Routes registered records by provenance and layer after normalizing their shape.
Forks built-in records into user space on update while keeping the same id and recording forkedFrom.
Querying supports layer, family, provenance, extends, tag, and text filters, then sorts by layer metadata rank and name.
Inheritance resolution builds a lineage chain and merges defaults, slots, variables, and tags from ancestors into the requested type.
Slot validation checks accepted layers, families, and tags against a candidate type.
Subscriptions use a Set of listeners and a suppressNotify flag for batched registration.
standouts: One Type shape is used across tokens, atoms, blocks, systems, surfaces, docs, and templates.
User records shadow built-ins because get checks USER before BUILTIN.
Templates are represented as Type records at layer template but stored in their own map.
conventions: Uses plain browser JavaScript without imports or module exports.
Uses Maps for internal registries and arrays for public query/all results.
Uses shallow object spreads for normalization, patching, and inheritance merges.
Uses optional chaining for acceptance checks.
Swallows localStorage and listener errors with empty catch blocks.
Uses kebab-case-oriented ids by convention but does not enforce the format.
concerns: Empty catch blocks hide storage parse/write failures and listener exceptions.
registerMany persists user types and templates even when all registered records are built-ins.
update of a built-in preserves the original id, causing the user fork to shadow the built-in in get/all query behavior.
register does not validate layer names, family names, slot schema, variable schema, or duplicate ids.
resolve does not surface inheritance cycles; lineage silently stops when a repeated id is seen.
notes: This file is the browser-side registry core for Type records, including persistence, lookup, filtering, inheritance merging, embedding checks, and change notifications.
questions: Should built-in forks intentionally keep the same id rather than receiving a distinct user id?
Should storage and listener failures be observable to diagnostics or callers?
Should invalid layers, malformed slots, or duplicate records be rejected during registration?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a shared Type registry with layers, inheritance, embedding slots, templates, persistence for non-built-ins, and a public browser API.
intention_signals: It seems intended to let multiple ConceptV authoring surfaces share one composable Type model while allowing user, Vi-authored, imported, and template records to be added at runtime.
novelty: The file combines design-system layer taxonomy, template parameterization, inheritance flattening, slot-based embedding constraints, and browser-local extensibility in one registry surface.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral Type registry substrate: layered type metadata, record normalization, local persistence boundaries, inheritance merging, slot acceptance logic, querying, and subscription hooks that a fused system could retain as shared registry mechanics.
summary_for_embedding: Browser JavaScript registry core for ConceptV Type records spanning token through template layers, with localStorage persistence, filtering, inheritance resolution, slot validation, and window.CV_REGISTRY export.
intention_for_embedding: This was for centralizing composable design/document Type definitions so authoring surfaces can register, query, resolve, embed, persist, and observe them consistently.

symbols.does: Loads persisted user and template records from localStorage into their maps.
symbols.description: Reads the configured user and template storage keys, parses any JSON arrays found there, and inserts each record by id into USER or TEMPLATES. Parse and access failures are ignored.

symbols.does: Persists current USER map values to localStorage.
symbols.description: Serializes all USER records as an array and stores them under the user-types storage key. Storage failures are ignored.

symbols.does: Persists current TEMPLATES map values to localStorage.
symbols.description: Serializes all template records as an array and stores them under the templates storage key. Storage failures are ignored.

symbols.does: Normalizes and stores one type record in the appropriate registry map.
symbols.description: Applies normalize, rejects missing ids with a console warning, then routes built-ins to BUILTIN, templates to TEMPLATES, and other records to USER. Unless silent, it persists non-built-ins and emits a registry notification.

symbols.does: Registers multiple type records as a batch.
symbols.description: Temporarily suppresses notifications while registering each record silently. After the loop, it persists user types and templates once and emits one notification unless the batch call is silent.

symbols.does: Patches an existing type and re-registers the result.
symbols.description: Looks up the current record by id and returns null when absent. Built-ins are converted into user-provenance fork records with updated timestamps and version incrementing; non-built-ins are shallow-merged with the patch, timestamped, versioned, and registered.

symbols.does: Removes a non-built-in record by id.
symbols.description: Refuses to remove records present in BUILTIN. Deletes matching ids from USER and TEMPLATES, then persists and notifies when something was removed and the call is not silent.

symbols.does: Returns the record for an id from the registry maps.
symbols.description: Handles falsy ids by returning null. Lookup order is USER, then BUILTIN, then TEMPLATES, so user records can shadow built-ins with the same id.

symbols.does: Returns all built-in, user, and template records as one array.
symbols.description: Concatenates current values from BUILTIN, USER, and TEMPLATES in that order. It does not deduplicate ids across maps.

symbols.does: Filters registry records by metadata and text search.
symbols.description: Starts from all records, applies optional filters for layer, layers, family, families, provenance, extends id, tags, and lowercased text search across id, name, description, and tags. Returns the filtered records sorted by byLayerThenName.

symbols.does: Compares two type records by layer rank and display name.
symbols.description: Uses LAYER_INFO rank values, defaulting unknown layers to 99. When layer ranks match, it compares names with localeCompare.

symbols.does: Flattens a type with its ancestor records into one merged record.
symbols.description: Builds the lineage for an id and returns null if no chain exists. It reduces ancestors into the requested type so leaf fields win while defaults, slots, variables, and tags are merged, then stamps lineage, id, name, and provenance from the requested type.

symbols.does: Returns a type's inheritance chain from self to ancestors.
symbols.description: Starts at get(id), follows extends links, and accumulates records until there is no parent or a repeated id is encountered. The returned order is self, parent, grandparent, and onward.

symbols.does: Finds direct children of a type id.
symbols.description: Scans all registry records and returns records whose extends value equals the provided id.

symbols.does: Finds recursive descendants of a type id.
symbols.description: Creates an output array and uses the nested walk helper to traverse children depth-first. Each child is appended before its own descendants are explored.

symbols.does: Recursively appends children and deeper descendants for a parent id.
symbols.description: Defined inside descendants, this helper loops over children(pid), pushes each child into the shared output array, and recurses with the child's id.

symbols.does: Merges two variable arrays by variable key.
symbols.description: Builds a Map keyed by variable key, inserts variables from the first array, then overlays variables from the second array on matching keys. The result is returned as an array of merged Map values.

symbols.does: Checks whether a type satisfies a slot's accept constraints.
symbols.description: Returns false for missing slot or type. It evaluates optional accepted layers, families, and tags, requiring the type to match each specified constraint group.

symbols.does: Returns all registry records accepted by a slot.
symbols.description: Filters all records through accepts(slot, t), producing candidate types that can be embedded into that slot according to its accept constraints.

symbols.does: Fills defaults and timestamps for a type-like input object.
symbols.description: Creates a complete Type-shaped object with default layer, family, description, slots, defaults, variables, tags, provenance, icon, timestamps, version, forkedFrom, and runtime. It preserves render when present.

symbols.does: Adds a listener and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: Stores the provided function in the listeners Set. The returned closure removes the same function from the Set.

symbols.does: Calls registered listeners unless notifications are suppressed.
symbols.description: Exits early when suppressNotify is true. Otherwise it iterates the listeners Set and invokes each listener, ignoring listener errors.
